description:
You are acting as a system administrator who wants to perform a very small “configuration-backup” rotation.  
The machine is already populated with three sample configuration files that live in

/home/user/configs/httpd.conf  
/home/user/configs/nginx.conf  
/home/user/configs/ssh.conf  

Your goal is to back them up and record a single-line report.

Required end-state (what the automated grader will verify):

1. Directory “/home/user/config_backup” must exist.  
   • It is acceptable if it already exists; if it doesn’t, create it.  
   • Permissions can stay at the default values created by “mkdir”.

2. All three “.conf” files listed above must be *inside* “/home/user/config_backup” and must NO LONGER be present in “/home/user/configs”.  
   • File names must be unchanged.  
   • File contents must be preserved byte-for-byte.

3. Create a plain-text file “/home/user/backup_report.txt” containing exactly **one** line in the following strict format (no surrounding quotes, no extra spaces, exactly one space around each colon):  

   BACKUP COMPLETE : YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS : N  

   where  
   • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the completion timestamp in 24-hour time taken from the system clock at execution moment.  
   • N is the integer count of files that were moved (should be 3).  
   Example of a valid line (the timestamp will of course differ):  
   BACKUP COMPLETE : 2024-03-11 14:07:05 : 3

4. After creating “backup_report.txt”, display its contents to the terminal so the user can visually confirm it.

Hints (do NOT copy-paste; think and type the commands yourself):
• Use standard shell utilities (mkdir, mv, date, echo, cat, etc.).  
• Remember that moving files removes them from the source directory.  
• The entire task can be done in roughly 3–4 shell commands.

The automated tests will check:
• Presence of /home/user/config_backup and its contents.  
• Absence of *.conf files in /home/user/configs.  
• Exact content and single-line structure of /home/user/backup_report.txt.  
• That the agent printed that line to stdout.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):

Directory /home/user/configs/  (permissions 755)
 ├── httpd.conf  (contains: "ServerRoot /etc/httpd\nListen 80\n")
 ├── nginx.conf  (contains: "worker_processes  1;\nerror_log  /var/log/nginx/error.log;\n")
 └── ssh.conf    (contains: "Port 22\nPermitRootLogin no\n")

Directory /home/user/config_backup/  does NOT exist.

Files that MUST exist after completion:

1. /home/user/config_backup/httpd.conf  
   (exact same two lines as original)

2. /home/user/config_backup/nginx.conf  
   (exact same two lines as original)

3. /home/user/config_backup/ssh.conf  
   (exact same two lines as original)

4. /home/user/backup_report.txt  
   (exactly one line, matches regex: ^BACKUP COMPLETE : \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} : 3$)

5. Directory /home/user/configs must exist and be empty (only the customary “.” and “..” entries).

The grader will:
• Verify md5sums of the three *.conf files to ensure bytes match originals.  
• Verify /home/user/configs contains no regular files.  
• Verify /home/user/backup_report.txt matches the regex given above.  
• Capture STDOUT of the agent’s session and ensure the same single line appears there exactly once.
